default_activation_rule: Enable only the default built-in skills for normal sf6ingest maintainer work. Conditional built-ins and external APM skills require task-scoped need and reviewed repo boundaries.

builtin_default_skills.reason: Core Hermes operation and repo-local orchestration.
Provider execution coordination under Hermes direction.
Read and inspect repository files before drafting repo artifacts.
Issue triage and roadmap-oriented maintainer workflow.
Draft PR workflow support for reviewed repo artifacts.
Review drafting for issue-scoped repository changes.
Repository issue and PR management support.
Plans and handoffs for small reviewed changes.
Review handoff and PR readiness support.
Small exploratory maintainer investigations before scoped PRs.
Bounded delegation for larger repo-local maintainer work.

builtin_conditional_skills.reason: Useful for scoped source/video review tasks.
Useful when a reviewed task explicitly needs document or image text extraction.
Useful for explicit source monitoring or freshness review work.
Useful for scoped experimental evaluation work.
Only for a later reviewed long-running workflow that enables Kanban.
Only for a later reviewed long-running workflow that enables Kanban.
Useful for scoped local exploration, not default maintainer operation.

external_apm_skills.reason: Maintainer-local operator instructions for the selected SymPy backend.

deferred_candidates.reason: Potentially useful for heavier symbolic math, but not needed for the initial SymPy route.
deferred_candidates.required_before_adoption: Reviewed issue with pinned dependency, task scope, and no formula authority promotion.

deferred_candidates.reason: Potentially useful for later analysis, but broad scientific bundles would add context noise.
deferred_candidates.required_before_adoption: Reviewed issue with selected subset, pinned dependency, and validator coverage.

deferred_candidates.reason: MCP defaults need server allowlists and separate state handling.
deferred_candidates.required_before_adoption: Reviewed MCP policy with safe config, state boundary, and validator coverage.

deferred_candidates.reason: Gateway or bot operation expands auth and network exposure.
deferred_candidates.required_before_adoption: Reviewed gateway policy with allowlists, auth boundary, and fail-closed behavior.

deferred_candidates.reason: Long-running orchestration state is local and should not be enabled by default.
deferred_candidates.required_before_adoption: Reviewed lifecycle policy for durable workers, state promotion, and rollback.

deferred_candidates.reason: Provider memory may improve recall but adds privacy and state-governance concerns.
deferred_candidates.required_before_adoption: Reviewed memory provider issue with data handling and non-canonical output rules.

deferred_candidates.reason: ADR-0002 defers public skill distribution while private Hermes-first operation is stabilized.
deferred_candidates.required_before_adoption: Later architecture decision to remove, relocate, or reactivate public adapter surfaces.

forbidden_categories.reason: Personal app access is unrelated to SF6 maintainer operation.
Creative generation adds tool temptation and is not default maintainer work.
Unrelated gaming skills do not support SF6 Knowledge Agent Kit maintenance.
Media generation/playback is unrelated to repo artifact maintenance.
Personal messaging access is outside repo-local maintainer scope.
Red-team skills need a separate explicit safety scope.
Smart-home actions are unrelated and side-effectful.
Posting skills are outside repo maintenance and side-effectful.
Finance workflows are not part of SF6 maintainer operation.
Health workflows are not part of SF6 maintainer operation.
Training/serving workflows add unrelated runtime and infra risk.
Mutating external workspaces must require an explicit reviewed task.
Sensitive local profile data must remain outside repo artifacts.
Raw transcripts and runtime state are non-canonical local data.
